compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic collapse of vegetative matter. P1 (Yellow Pot) is devoid of visible foliage. P3 (Black Pot) has lost its primary anchor (White Rabbit) and the Pothos specimen is no longer visible. P2/P4 (Black Pot) shows only residual debris.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil surface; no signs of succulent growth.
plant_audit.p2_p4: Empty soil surface; presence of eggshell fragments suggests an attempted nutrient intervention that failed.
plant_audit.p3: Empty soil surface; the scale anchor (rabbit) has been removed.

biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in the black pot indicates a failed attempt at soil amendment. The removal of the scale anchor (rabbit) indicates a complete clearing of the desk space.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state showed a single green leaf in the black pot and a rabbit anchor.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Gradual chlorosis and wilting of the remaining leaf; loss of structural integrity.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Total disappearance of all plant matter. The biome is now effectively sterile.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The specimens have undergone total senescence or removal. There is no biological activity remaining in the pots.
anomalies: Total loss of all registered plant specimens
Accumulation of non-organic debris (eggshells) in empty pots
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a complete systemic failure of the indoor desk biome. Over the observed period, the specimens transitioned from a state of decline to total absence. The removal of the scale anchor (the white rabbit) and the presence of eggshell fragments suggest a failed attempt to revive the plants before their eventual disappearance. The current state is one of total biological vacancy.
